summary: Tested Product Conditional Visibility (4th option) and IntakeSchemaBuilder multi-condition visibility. Found and fixed a CRITICAL backend bug where @router.get('/products') decorator was misplaced on helper function _eval_product_conditions instead of get_products. Fixed TypeScript compilation error in ProductEditor.tsx. All 14 backend tests pass. All frontend features working end-to-end.

critical.issue: CRITICAL BUG FIXED: @router.get('/products') decorator was applied to _eval_product_conditions() helper function instead of get_products() in /app/backend/routes/store.py. This caused the endpoint to return 422 requiring body params (rule_set, customer) instead of the product list. FIX APPLIED: Moved the decorator to the correct async def get_products() function.

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED: Missing visibility_conditions: null in initial form state. Caused TypeScript compilation error: 'Property visibility_conditions is missing in type but required in type ProductFormData'.
React key warning: Each child in a list should have a unique 'key' prop. Check the render method of Primitive.div. It was passed a child from ProductForm. Non-blocking but should be fixed.

action_items: Fix React key prop warning in ProductForm.tsx (Primitive.div mapping without key props) - minor but worth fixing
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL: @router.get('/products') decorator was on _eval_product_conditions() not get_products() in store.py - completely broke the public store products endpoint. FIX APPLIED in store.py.
ProductEditor.tsx was missing visibility_conditions: null in initial state causing TypeScript compilation error. FIX APPLIED.
Backend _eval_product_conditions function is now a pure helper (no route decorator) - correctly evaluates AND/OR logic, all operators (equals, not_equals, contains, not_contains, empty, not_empty)
evaluateVisibilityRule in utils.tsx correctly handles both legacy {depends_on, operator, value} and new {logic, conditions: [...]} format with circular dep prevention
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_UI_Conditional_Save product with visibility_conditions during UI testing. Multiple TEST_ prefixed products created and deactivated during backend testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two major bugs were fixed this iteration: (1) CRITICAL: @router.get('/products') was on _eval_product_conditions() not get_products() - now fixed. (2) ProductEditor.tsx missing visibility_conditions: null in initial state - now fixed. All conditional visibility features are working: 4 radio options, condition builder with field/operator/value, AND/OR toggle, empty/not_empty hides value input, max 4 conditions. Intake multi-condition visibility also works with all operators. Backend API correctly filters conditional products. Admin always sees all products.

critical_decorator_bug.description: In /app/backend/routes/store.py, a helper function _eval_product_conditions() was introduced to evaluate conditional visibility rules. The @router.get('/products') decorator was accidentally placed ABOVE this helper function instead of above the async def get_products() function below it. FastAPI registered _eval_product_conditions as the GET /products handler, causing it to require rule_set and customer body params. get_products was never registered.
critical_decorator_bug.fix: Removed @router.get('/products') from _eval_product_conditions() and placed it on async def get_products() instead.
critical_decorator_bug.impact: Entire public store product listing was broken - no products were returned to any customer

typescript_compilation_error.description: ProductEditor.tsx had an initial form state object missing the visibility_conditions field that was added to ProductFormData interface. TypeScript strict mode caught this as a type error preventing compilation.
typescript_compilation_error.fix: Added visibility_conditions: null to the initial form state object in ProductEditor.tsx
